Hr. fF. 842( 4), TRA 19-9-89 Bre Wahre fran ter on athe wea SH ATLA. 700( 1), Mba 26-11-91, ALP. 438(3), athe 21-4-92 AR aaP. 509( a1), atte 15~6-94 aro ayia fara rat ert MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (Railway Board) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 4th July, 1996 G.S.R. 270(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (c), (e), (f) and (g) of sub-section (2) of Section 30 of the Railway Claims Tribunal Act, 1987 (54 of 1987), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, further to amend the Railway Claims Tribunal (Procedure) Rules, 1989, namely :-— — (1) These Rules may be called the Railway Claims Tribunal (Procedure) Amendment Rules, 1996. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Under Rule 5 of the Railway Claims Tribunal (Procedure) Rules, 1989, Form II for preferring claims for death or injury as a result of train accident or untoward incident shall be amended as under : (1) The heading shall appear in Part III of the form. (2) In Part II, line 2 of para one of application, for the words “in a railway accident” , the words “in accident to train or untoward incident” may be substitated 3. In part III, last line of the para two, for the wods “in the railway accident”, the words “accident to train or untoward incident”, may be substituted. 4, In part ID, para three, the words “or untoward incident” may be added after the words “in the accident.” Item 6 under para three may be renumbered as item 6(a) and another item 6(b) may be added as under : “6(b) Brief particulars of the untoward incident indicating the date and place of the untoward incident.” 6. In item 7, para three, the words “ticket/pass number” may be substituted with the words “ticket/pass/ platform ticket number.” In item 11, para three, the words “to the train” may be added after the words “accident.” In item 17, para threo, after the words “occurrence of the accident”, the words “to the train or unto- ward incident” may be added. 9. In part IIL, item (b) of declaration, the words “injury/death” may be substituted with the words “injury/death/loss of luggage.” [No. 89/TC(RCT)/1-5] H. C. PUNIA, Executive Director/Public Grievances Note : The Principal rules were published in the Gazette of India vide GSR 842(E) dated 19-9-1989 and subse- quently amended by GSR 700(E) dated 26-11-1991, GSR 438(E) dated 21-4-1992 and GSR 509(E) dated 15-6-1994.
